Operation 'captureScreenshotGet' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects, not_actionable, poor_ai_interpretability. Original summary and description are too generic and lack actionable details. The improved version specifies: (1) what is being captured (website screenshot), (2) the HTTP method and parameter style, (3) key input parameters available (formats, viewport, full-page, geo-location, custom code, S3), (4) what is returned (image or URL), and (5) authentication requirement. This enables AI agents to understand when to use this operation and what capabilities it provides.

data.current_description: Capture a screenshot of a URL using GET request with query parameters
data.summary_suggestion: Capture website screenshot via GET request
data.description_suggestion: Captures a screenshot of a specified website URL using HTTP GET with query parameters. Accepts parameters for output format (PNG, JPEG, WebP), viewport dimensions, full-page capture, geo-location emulation, custom CSS/JS injection, and S3 storage options. Returns the screenshot image in the requested format or a URL to the captured image. Requires valid API access key for authentication.
data.reasoning: Original summary and description are too generic and lack actionable details. The improved version specifies: (1) what is being captured (website screenshot), (2) the HTTP method and parameter style, (3) key input parameters available (formats, viewport, full-page, geo-location, custom code, S3), (4) what is returned (image or URL), and (5) authentication requirement. This enables AI agents to understand when to use this operation and what capabilities it provides.
